pages.raw_text:
Editor Press-Please permit me
word in regard to the safety Of
the public" that the Washington
Water Power Co. prates about. By
standing at some of the railroad
crossings when a Washington
Water Power car is crossing and
noting how long it takes the car to
cross the tracks, especially when
there is snow or mud on the rails,
the public will readily understand
how to account for the accidents
that happen at the railroad cross
ings. 1t is like taking a dive into
eternity" to make one of these
trips. The reason for this is the
cheese pairing policy of the COM
pany in buying small, light cars
with weak motors, all the power
of which is needed to make the
speed under favorable conditions.
Compare them with the cars of the
Traction Co. and you will notice
that the latter cars have power
enough to give them momentum
sufficient to take them across the
railroad tracks in case a trolley
comes off, or progress is impeded
by snow or mud on the tracks.


1t Is a rare thing to hear Of a
wreck on the Traction lines. They
employ experienced men.


The w. w. p. Co. is proud of its
men, service and equipment. But
what has 1t got to be proud Of? Is
